name: Lincolnshire Plum Bread
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Prunes; cut into sultana-size pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Using a food mixer or processor, or your hands, mix and knead all the ingredients except the dried fruits to form a smooth and elastic dough.
2. Cover and leave to rise until doubled in size—about 1 hour in a warm room.
3. Knock back the risen dough and knead briefly, gradually working in the dried fruits until evenly distributed.
4. Divide the dough into two equal portions. Shape each portion into small loaves and place them into greased and lined loaf tins (each weighing about 1 lb).
5. Cover and leave to prove (rise) until puffy and light.
6. Bake on a preheated baking sheet at 375°F (190°C) gas mark 5 for 40-50 minutes.
7. Remove the loaves from the tins and return them to the oven for an additional 10 minutes or so, as necessary. The bread will sound hollow when tapped on the base if it is properly cooked.
8. Makes 2 small loaves.
